reference.scope: X-RAY CRYSTALLOGRAPHY (2.60 ANGSTROMS) OF 1-317 IN COMPLEXES WITH ENDOGENOUS AGONIST ADENOSINE AND SYNTHETIC AGONIST NECA

citation.name: Nat. Genet.
citation.title: Characterization of single-nucleotide polymorphisms in coding regions of human genes.

comment.text: Receptor for adenosine (By similarity). The activity of this receptor is mediated by G proteins which activate adenylyl cyclase (By similarity).

Interacts (via cytoplasmic C-terminal domain) with USP4; the interaction is direct (PubMed:16339847). May interact with DRD4 (PubMed:18832607). Interacts with NECAB2 (PubMed:17689978). Interacts (via cytoplasmic C-terminal domain) with GAS2L2; interaction enhances receptor-mediated adenylyl cyclase activity (By similarity).
Colocalizes with GAS2L2 at neuronal processes.
The cytoplasmic C-terminal domain is necessary for targeting the non-ubiquitinated form of this protein to the cell surface.
Ubiquitinated. Deubiquitinated by USP4; leading to stabilization and expression at the cell surface.
Belongs to the G-protein coupled receptor 1 family.

property.value: Group enriched (bone marrow, brain, lymphoid tissue)
Acute encephalopathy with biphasic seizures and late reduced diffusion
4 GO annotations based on evolutionary models
Expressed in putamen and 94 other cell types or tissues
P:adenylate cyclase-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway
P:adenylate cyclase-modulating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway
P:G protein-coupled adenosine receptor signaling pathway
P:negative regulation of alpha-beta T cell activation
P:negative regulation of cell population proliferation
P:negative regulation of neuron apoptotic process
P:phospholipase C-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway
P:positive regulation of acetylcholine secretion, neurotransmission
P:positive regulation of apoptotic signaling pathway
P:positive regulation of circadian sleep/wake cycle, sleep
P:positive regulation of ERK1 and ERK2 cascade
P:positive regulation of long-term synaptic potentiation
P:positive regulation of synaptic transmission, GABAergic
P:positive regulation of synaptic transmission, glutamatergic
P:presynaptic modulation of chemical synaptic transmission
